title: Space Tech Briefing — June 12, 2026
summary:
Good morning, space enthusiasts and future trailblazers! This is your daily dose of the cosmos, live from Space Tech Central, on this magnificent June 12, 2026. Get ready to launch into the latest and greatest from orbit and beyond!

First up, a fascinating new development straight from the International Space Station! Our very own French astronaut, Sophie Adenot, was hard at work on June 10. She completed the third session of the groundbreaking PhysioTool experiment. This incredible project was developed specifically for her Epsilon mission by the French space agency, CNES. And here’s the cool part: it's being operated directly from their facilities in Toulouse, France! It's a fantastic example of how Earth-based teams are directly controlling and monitoring vital research happening hundreds of miles above us. Truly a testament to global collaboration and remote control in space!

And speaking of incredible missions, as we wait for today's new launches, let’s look back at a major recent development that's still buzzing across the space community. We're talking about Artemis III! Just a few days ago, on June 9, NASA announced the crew for this pivotal mission. Among the four astronauts selected, we have the European Space Agency's very own Luca Parmitano, an Italian astronaut, joining three American colleagues. This is huge news! Artemis III is designed for a two-week flight in low Earth orbit. Its critical mission? To test the crucial docking procedures with the future Human Landing System, or HLS, that will eventually take us back to the lunar surface. This mission is a vital stepping stone in NASA's broader Artemis strategy, which aims to land humans on the Moon again by 2028 with Artemis IV. Luca Parmitano's selection is a proud moment for international space cooperation and for Italy!

What an exciting time to be alive, witnessing these incredible leaps in space exploration! That’s all for your morning briefing. Stay curious, keep looking up, and we'll connect again soon for more cosmic updates! Have a stellar day!
